name: Shift-and-Mask Bit Count + Selection Sort
summary: Count each number's 1-bits by testing the lowest bit and shifting it off, then selection-sort by (bit count, value).
visualizationIntent: init: show the unsorted array with an empty 1-bit key row beneath it. count (phase 1): pointer i walks the array; for each number the inner loop tests the lowest bit (num & 1) then shifts it off (num >> 1), and the key row fills in that number's 1-bit count. sort (phase 2): grow a sorted prefix to the left of i; scan j across the unsorted right, tracking best (the smallest key so far); the compared element (j) and the best are highlighted. update: best jumps to j when a smaller (bit-count, then value) key is found. move: swap the best element and its key into position i. return: the array is sorted by 1-bit count, then by value.

lineExplanations.3: n is how many integers we must sort.
lineExplanations.4: bit_counts will hold each integer's number of 1-bits, in the same order as arr.
lineExplanations.5: Start the counting index i at 0.
lineExplanations.6: Phase 1 â€” loop over every integer to count its 1-bits.
lineExplanations.7: Copy the current integer into num; we destroy num while counting, so arr is left intact.
lineExplanations.8: count accumulates this integer's 1-bits, starting at 0.
lineExplanations.9: Keep going while num still has bits left to inspect.
lineExplanations.10: num & 1 is 1 when the lowest bit is set; add that to count.
lineExplanations.11: Shift num right by one, discarding the bit we just counted.
lineExplanations.12: Record this integer's total 1-bit count in bit_counts.
lineExplanations.13: Move on to the next integer.
lineExplanations.14: Phase 2 â€” reset i to 0 to begin the selection sort.
lineExplanations.15: For each position i, find the element that belongs there.
lineExplanations.16: Assume the element already at i has the smallest key so far.
lineExplanations.17: Start scanning from the next position, j.
lineExplanations.18: Scan the unsorted region looking for a smaller key.
lineExplanations.19: Does arr[j] have fewer 1-bits than the current best?
lineExplanations.20: If so, j becomes the new best.
lineExplanations.21: Otherwise, does arr[j] have the SAME number of 1-bits as best?
lineExplanations.22: On a tie in 1-bits, is arr[j] the smaller value?
lineExplanations.23: If so, j becomes the new best (ties are broken by value).
lineExplanations.24: Advance the scan to the next element.
lineExplanations.26: Move the best element into position i.
lineExplanations.27: Put the saved value where best was â€” the swap is done.
lineExplanations.28: Save bit_counts[i] so the key row stays aligned with arr.
lineExplanations.29: Move best's key into position i.
lineExplanations.30: Put the saved key where best was â€” the key swap is done.
lineExplanations.31: Position i is settled; advance to the next position to fill.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: â€” the standard wrapper class the platform expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def sortByBits(self, arr): â€” arr is the list of integers to sort.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(arr) counts the elements in the list.
syntaxExplanations.6: while i < n: repeats while i is still a valid index.
syntaxExplanations.7: arr[i] reads the element at index i.
syntaxExplanations.9: while num > 0: loops until every bit of num has been consumed.
syntaxExplanations.10: num & 1 keeps only the lowest bit; + adds it (0 or 1) to count.
syntaxExplanations.11: num >> 1 shifts all bits one position to the right.
syntaxExplanations.12: .append(count) adds count to the end of bit_counts.
syntaxExplanations.14: i = 0 resets the index for the second pass.
syntaxExplanations.15: while i < n: the outer selection-sort loop.
syntaxExplanations.16: best = i â€” best holds the index of the smallest key found so far.
syntaxExplanations.17: j = i + 1 â€” j scans the still-unsorted region.
syntaxExplanations.18: while j < n: scans to the end of the array.
syntaxExplanations.20: best = j updates the best index.
syntaxExplanations.21: elif runs only when the first if was false (equal 1-bit counts).
syntaxExplanations.23: best = j updates best on a value tie-break.
syntaxExplanations.25: temp = arr[i] stores a value before the swap.
syntaxExplanations.28: temp_bits = bit_counts[i] stores the key before its swap.
syntaxExplanations.30: bit_counts[best] = temp_bits completes the key swap.
syntaxExplanations.31: i = i + 1 moves to the next position to fill.
syntaxExplanations.32: return arr hands back the sorted list.
